title: Mnesithea perforata
taxonomy: Mnesithea perforata is classified as a species of grass within the family Poaceae, commonly known as the true grasses or cereal family. It belongs to the tribe Andropogoneae, a group of grasses that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Mnesithea is a part of the subfamily Panicoideae, which includes various species of grasses that are native to Africa and Asia. Mnesithea perforata is a type of grass that is known to grow in a variety of habitats, including grasslands, savannas, and open woodlands. Its classification reflects its evolutionary relationships with other grasses within the Poaceae family.
